subject: Chemistry
solution:
To determine the ion(s) present in very small quantity in the solution, we need to consider the chemical reaction that occurs when silver nitrate reacts with potassium iodide. The reaction between AgNO3 and KI is a double replacement reaction and can be represented as follows:

AgNO3 + KI → AgI + KNO3

Millimoles of AgNO3 = 25

Millimoles of KI = 25 × 1.05=26.25

∴KI is in excess & AgI forms negatively charged colloid. (Some Ag+remains in solution) lons Ag+& F-are therefore, present in very small quantity.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
2H3AsO4+5H2S→Conc.HClAs2S5+8H2O

2 moles of Arsenic Acid → 1 mole of Arsenic Pentasulphide

1 moles of Arsenic Acid → 1/2 mole of Arsenic Pentasulphide

Molar mass of H3AsO4=141; Molar mass of As2S5=308

∴ Number of moles of H3AsO4=35.5141=0.25

∴  Number of moles of As2S5=0.252=0.125 mol

subject: Chemistry
solution:
$\mathrm{Mg}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{MgCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2$
Volume $\mathrm{H}_2$ evolved $=220 \mathrm{ml}$
Mole of $\mathrm{H}_2=\frac{220 \times 10^{-3}}{22.4}=$ mole of Mg used
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \text { Mass of Mg used } & =\frac{220 \times 10^{-3}}{22.4} \times 24 \\
& =235.7 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{gm} \\
& =235.7 \mathrm{mg}
\end{aligned}$

∴ nH2SO4=x,nHCl=2x

⇒nH+=4x

Moles of H+ formed =4x

Moles of OH- from 16 moles of NaOH=16

4x=16 (for complete neutralisation)

x=4

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Zinc reacts with hydrochloric acid to give hydrogen and zinc chloride.

Zn + 2HCl → ZnCl2 + H2↑

The molar mass of Zn is 65.4 g mol-1

Molar volume od H2 at STP = 22.7 L

Number of moles of Zn =11.565.4= Number of moles of  H2evolved 

Volume of H2 = 11.565.4 × 22.7 L = 3.99 L ≈ 4 L

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Let the formula of metal chloride be MClx

Mass of 100 mL of vapours of MClx at STP =0.57 g

The mass of substance which occupies 22400 ml of volume at STP is molar mass(M)

∴ 100×M22400=0.57

∴ Molar mass of MClx=127.68 g mol–1

The mass percentage of chlorine=mass of chlorinemass of the compound×100

% of Cl=35.5x×100127.68=55

⇒ x=2

∴ Formula of metal chloride is MCl2

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For A: Molar mass of $H_2O = 18$ g/mol. Moles of $H_2O = \dfrac{1.8 \times 10^{-3}}{18} = 10^{-4}$. Since each $H_2O$ molecule has $3$ atoms, the number of atoms = $3 \times 10^{-4} \times N_A$.

For B: Molar mass of $H_2SO_4 = 98$ g/mol. Moles of $H_2SO_4 = \dfrac{9.8 \times 10^{-3}}{98} = 10^{-4}$. Since each $H_2SO_4$ molecule has $7$ atoms, the number of atoms = $7 \times 10^{-4} \times N_A$.

For C: Molar mass of $C = 12$ g/mol. Moles of $C = \dfrac{1.8 \times 10^{-3}}{12} = 1.5 \times 10^{-4}$. The number of atoms = $1.5 \times 10^{-4} \times N_A$.

For D: Molar mass of $NaCl = 58.5$ g/mol. Moles of $NaCl = \dfrac{5.85 \times 10^{-3}}{58.5} = 10^{-4}$. Since each $NaCl$ formula unit has $2$ atoms, the number of atoms = $2 \times 10^{-4} \times N_A$.

Thus, the correct matching is A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I.

Answer: A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Mass percentage of $\text{Fe} = 69.9\%$

Mass percentage of $\text{O} = 100\% - 69.9\% = 30.1\%$

Number of moles of $\text{Fe} = \dfrac{69.9}{56} = 1.248$

Number of moles of $\text{O} = \dfrac{30.1}{16} = 1.881$

Molar ratio of $\text{Fe} : \text{O} = 1.248 : 1.881$

Dividing by the smallest value, the ratio is $\dfrac{1.248}{1.248} : \dfrac{1.881}{1.248} = 1 : 1.5$

Multiplying by $2$ to get the simplest whole number ratio, we get $2 : 3$.

The empirical formula is $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$.

Answer: $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$

subject: Chemistry
solution: Gay-Lussac's Law proposes a relationship between the combining volumes of gases with respect to the reactants and gaseous products. According to this law, when gases react together to produce gaseous products, the volumes of reactants and products bear a simple whole number ratio with each other, provided volumes are measured at same temperature and pressure.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For (A):

Formula of cyclohexane is $C_6H_{12}$.

Number of atoms per molecule = $6 + 12 = 18$.

Total number of atoms in $2$ moles = $2 \times 18 \times N_A = 36 N_A$.

For (B):

Formula of sucrose is $C_{12}H_{22}O_{11}$.

Molar mass of sucrose = $342$ g/mol.

Number of moles = $\dfrac{684}{342} = 2$ moles.

Number of atoms per molecule = $12 + 22 + 11 = 45$.

Total number of atoms = $2 \times 45 \times N_A = 90 N_A$.

For (C):

Formula of dihydrogen is $H_2$.

Number of moles at STP = $\dfrac{90.8}{22.7} = 4$ moles.

Number of atoms per molecule = $2$.

Total number of atoms = $4 \times 2 \times N_A = 8 N_A$.

Comparing the total number of atoms:

$90 N_A > 36 N_A > 8 N_A$

Therefore, the correct order is $B > A > C$.

Answer: $B>A>C$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Consider one mole of the oxide, then

Moles of M = 0.98;     Moles of O2- = 1

Let 'x' moles of M are in +3 oxidation state, then Moles of M2+ = 0.98-x

On doing charge balancing we get 

(0.98 - x)2 + 3x  = 2

Or 1.96 - 2x + 3x - 2 = 0

Or x = 0.04

 ∴ %of M3+=0.040.98×100=4.08%

solution:
NH2CONH2 + 2NaOH →2NH3 +Na2CO3

By, stoichiometry, 1 mol of urea gives 2 mol NH3

Moles of urea ==0.6 gm60 gm/mol=0.01 mol

So, 0.01 mole urea produce 0.02 mole NH3

To neutralize ammonia HCl is required

NH3 +HCl→ NH4Cl

By equation, it is clear that 1 mol of ammonia is neutralized by 1mol of HCl.

So, 0.02 mol of ammonia neutralized by 0.02 mol of HCl

∴Mole of HCl=0.02 mole

Equivalent of HCl=normality×volume=0.2 N×100 mL=0.02 equivalent

As n-factor of HCl is 1, so number of equivalents=number of moles=0.02 moles

Hence, option 3 is correct.
